title:
The Trump EPA just announced that it is repealing the 2009 endangerment finding, which is the scientific determination that climate pollution endangers public health and welfare.

This is not a… | Manish Bapna | 23 comments
description:
The Trump EPA just announced that it is repealing the 2009 endangerment finding, which is the scientific determination that climate pollution endangers public health and welfare.

This is not a technical change. It is core to nearly all federal climate protections for the past 15 years.

If this repeal is allowed to stand, it would be the single biggest attack in U.S. history on the federal government’s ability to protect people from climate pollution.

And make no mistake about what this means in real life: more dangerous heat, more wildfires, more extreme storms, more air pollution, and more health harms, with American families left to absorb the cost.

This move is part of a growing pattern of hostility toward the American public: stripping protections and making people less safe, all while billionaires profit.
NRDC will not let this stand. We will see them in court — and we will win. | 23 comments on LinkedIn

name: Costs & Impacts
title: Reckless Action by the EPA
description: Reckless Action by the EPA Today, President Trump and U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) Administrator Lee Zeldin announced the repeal of the EPA’s Endangerment Finding, which was the federal government’s scientific and legal foundation to reduce greenhouse gases and protect the air we breathe. Repealing the Endangerment Finding will harm the people and the environment in Boulder County. This reckless decision guts the EPA’s authority to reduce climate pollution. The decision burdens our community with dirtier air, worse health, and preventable deaths. This irresponsible move comes as communities like Boulder County face the constant threat of wildfires, floods, and ozone pollution. Right now, the American West is experiencing one of the warmest winters on record, and Colorado’s snowpack hovers at distressingly low levels. The devastation of climate change is here. While efforts to address climate change are being actively undermined by federal action, local governments must fight this global climate crisis with limited tools, while the costs, suffering, and stakes keep rising. We invite everyone in Boulder County to continue to join us in leading locally to advance climate solutions. We will not be discouraged. Commissioner Claire Levy Commissioner Marta Loachamin Commissioner Ashley Stolzmann Contact the Board of County Commissioners View […]

name: Costs & Impacts
title: “Devastating”: Trump EPA to Scrap Landmark Climate Finding in Pro-Fossil Fuel Deregulatory Push
description: In a victory for the fossil fuel industry, a set of Obama-era rules that required the federal government to regulate the emissions of six greenhouse gases is being reversed by the Trump administration. The changes would undo the legal basis of the fight against global warming, as well as remove industrial reporting obligations and roll back emissions standards for cars and trucks. Environmental engineer Gretchen Goldman helped author those emission standards while working for the Department of Transportation under the Biden administration. Now as the president of the Union of Concerned Scientists, she says their repeal will not only increase what drivers pay at the pump but also set U.S. innovation back on the world stage. “We’re really seeing the abdication of U.S. leadership on climate, and that has huge implications, both for our immediate ability to reduce heat-trapping emissions globally … but also in terms of our standing and contribution in the world.”

Former EPA Region IV Administrator Daniel Blackman is responding to EPA’s February 6 press release touting its PFAS record.

The agency’s statement fails to acknowledge its prolonged delays in finalizing and enforcing PFAS drinking water standards — delays that have real consequences for communities across the country.
